samples.gvd:
module right_shifter
(
    input clk,
    input d,
    output reg q
);
initial begin
    q = 8'b0; // Initialize q to 0 using an initial block.
end
always @(posedge clk) begin
    q <= (q >> 1); // Register is shifted to the right by 1 bit on each rising edge of the clock.
    q[7] <= d; // The most significant bit of the register is assigned the value of the input signal.
end
endmodule
samples.xml_description:
<module id="right_shifter">
    <description>Implement a right shifter.</description>
    <ports>
        <input id="clk">Clock.</input>
        <input id="d">Input signal to be right-shifted.</input>
        <output id="q" width_description="[7:0]">Output signal representing the result of the right shift operation.</output>
    </ports>
    <parameter_description>
        <param id="width" value="8">Width of the input and output signals.</param>
    </parameter_description>
    <logic_description>
        <type>sequential_logic</type>
        <description>Register is shifted to the right by 1 bit on each rising edge of the clock.</description>
        <assign>
            <lhs>q</lhs>
            <rhs>(q >> 1)</rhs>
        </assign>
        <assign>
            <lhs>q[7]</lhs>
            <rhs>d</rhs>
        </assign>
    </logic_description>
    <task>
        <description>Initialize the register to 0.</description>
        <sequential>
            <stmt>Set q to 0 using an initial block.</stmt>
            <initial>
                <assign>
                    <lhs>q</lhs>
                    <rhs>8'b0</rhs>
                </assign>
            </initial>
        </sequential>
    </task>
</module>
